hero.title: Chimney Sweep to the Founding of the RAF
hero.subtitle: The war that carried an Essex tradesman from Gallipoli to the birth of the Royal Air Force

media.alt: Maldon High Street, Essex — Archibald Coolbear's hometown
media.caption: Maldon, Essex — the small market town on the Blackwater estuary where Archibald Coolbear was born in 1892

media.alt: British soldiers in a trench at Cape Helles, Gallipoli, 1915, using a periscope to watch Turkish lines
media.caption: Cape Helles, 1915 — British troops in the Gallipoli trenches. Coolbear deployed with the Essex Regiment in May 1915

media.src: media/docs/Archibald Colbear RAF service record form official number 125257.jpg
media.alt: RAF service record form for Archibald Coolbear (filed under Colbear), showing enlistment, movements, and medical boards
media.caption: Coolbear's service record — documenting repeated hospitalisations for dysentery across France and England

media.alt: Blériot XI aircraft of the Royal Flying Corps lined up at Netheravon airfield
media.caption: RFC training airfield — Coolbear transferred to the Flying Corps in January 1918 and became an Instructor

media.alt: Ministry of Pensions card for Archibald Percy Cool Bear, RAF 125257, showing widow's claim
media.caption: Ministry of Pensions card — processed for Coolbear's widow in 1929, one year after his death at age 36
